name: Binary Tree Problems — LCA, Balance, Path Sum, Max Path

metadata.career_relevance: LCA is a top-5 tree interview question. Max Path Sum is a Google hard. Balanced/PathSum are warm-ups.

hook.real_world_problem:
// LCA: O(n) — if both found in same subtree, root of subtree is LCA
// Max Path: O(n) — max(0,child) to ignore negatives
hook.what_you_will_build: LCA, balance, max path, path sum, invert, serialize/deserialize.

concept_explanation.lca: Both non-null returns → current is LCA.
concept_explanation.max_path: Use max(0,child) to clip negatives; update global at each node.

key_takeaways: LCA: if root==p||root==q return root; both sides non-null → LCA
Balanced: return -1 from height on imbalance — O(n) not O(n²)
Max Path Sum: max(0,child) ignores negative branches
Path Sum: subtract at each node; check target==0 at leaf
Serialize: preorder with 'null' markers; deserialize with queue
reflection.next_topic_preview: Next: BST Operations — insert, delete, validate, kth smallest.
